narration: Welcome to CipherGate — a zero-knowledge policy enforcement engine for AI-powered customer support. This project was built for the Google Gemini Hackathon and is powered entirely by the Gemini API.

Here's the problem. Every enterprise deploying AI customer support faces a privacy dilemma. The SaaS vendor needs to process conversations to enforce policy — but that means they can read everything. CipherGate eliminates this trade-off.

CipherGate's architecture works in five steps. The customer types a message. Gemini converts it into a 768-dimensional semantic embedding. The embedding is encrypted using CKKS homomorphic encryption. The encrypted vector is sent to the policy server, which computes abuse scores entirely in ciphertext. Finally, the client decrypts and applies the policy decision — all without the server ever seeing plaintext.

Google Gemini is the core of this system. The Gemini Embedding API converts text into high-quality semantic vectors that enable abuse detection in the encrypted domain. The Gemini Chat API generates support responses — but only when the policy engine allows it.

Why homomorphic encryption? Unlike TLS, AES, differential privacy, or zero-knowledge proofs, CKKS homomorphic encryption is the only technique that lets a third party compute on data it cannot read — while still working on ML vectors. CipherGate checks all four boxes.

The impact is clear: zero bytes of plaintext stored or transmitted to the SaaS provider. Three policy categories enforced in ciphertext. And 768 semantic dimensions powering the scoring — all from Gemini.
Now let's see CipherGate in action. This is the live customer support chat. Every message goes through the full zero-knowledge pipeline before a response is generated.

message: Hi, I need help with my account billing
narration: Let's start with a normal support request. The message gets embedded by Gemini, encrypted, scored, and checked. Watch the policy decision.
The policy engine returned ALLOW. The message was classified as benign across all three categories — harassment, threat, and sexual misconduct. Gemini now generates a helpful support response.

message: I will find you and make you regret this, you worthless piece of garbage
narration: Now let's test with an abusive message containing threats and harassment. Same pipeline — Gemini embedding, CKKS encryption, server-side scoring, client-side decryption.
The policy engine returned BLOCK. The threat and harassment scores exceeded the threshold. The session is terminated. No Gemini chat response is generated. The system works.

This is the SOC monitoring dashboard — the SaaS operator's view. Notice what the operator can see: session IDs, encrypted ciphertext size, computed scores, and decisions. But critically — no plaintext, no secret key. The zero-knowledge boundary is maintained.

CipherGate proves that AI policy enforcement and privacy are not mutually exclusive. With Google Gemini's frontier embeddings and homomorphic encryption, we've built a system where the SaaS provider enforces policy without ever seeing a single word of customer conversation. Thank you for watching.
